ks1.objective: Run around and turn the cones to match your team — Volcanoes upright, Craters upside down.

lks2.objective: Scan and decide which cones to flip while moving — increase tempo and decision count.
successCriteria: Heads up while moving between cones
Targets the cones nearest to flip first

uks2.objective: Add footballs and weaker-foot dribbling while continuing the team flip-game.
successCriteria: Keeps the ball close while flipping cones
Uses weaker foot or different surfaces
Communicates with teammates to share zones

equipment.item: Giant cones (mix of upright and upside-down)

space.description: A large playing area with cones scattered both upright (volcanoes) and upside down (craters). Children divided into two teams.

value.name: Friendship
value.text: Teamwork. Friendship isn't just playing together — it's helping, supporting and communicating so everyone succeeds. Notice when teammates need help (turning a cone, covering space) and reinforce that working together is just as important as winning.

steps: Each team has a job: move around turning cones to match their team as quickly as possible.
Volcanoes turn cones the right way up. Craters turn them upside down.
The game is continuous — keep moving, scanning and making decisions throughout.
After a short period, shout 'Freeze!' and ask the children to stop where they are.
Count together how many volcanoes and craters there are, then announce the winning team for that round.
Restart quickly to keep tempo high and engagement strong.
progressions: Begin without footballs to build understanding of the game and rules.
Introduce new movement challenges — jumping, moving backwards.
Introduce footballs — challenge children to keep the ball close while moving.
Use different parts of the foot or weaker foot only when dribbling.
Change the teams so children can work with new friends and groups.
questions: How can your team cover the whole area together?
Which cones are quickest to flip?
How do you decide where to go next?

step.task: Complete a turn or trick before changing a cone
step.equipment: More cones = more decisions; or use different coloured cones with different rules
step.people: Adjust team sizes to create different challenges

base.showmanship: Use countdowns into each 'freeze' to build excitement and encourage a final burst of effort. Commentate on awareness of space and effective teamwork. Use children's names so parents can hear the positive reinforcement and understand what's being developed.
